description:
A project finance analyst at a solar IPP is preparing an investment memo for a 100 MW ground-mount PV plant near Daggett, CA. Before presenting to the investment committee, they must demonstrate the project's financial viability. Using PySAM (Python), model a 100 MW DC single-axis tracking utility PV project in Daggett, CA combining the Pvwattsv8 performance model and the Singleowner financial model.

System Design Parameters:
- DC system capacity: 100,000 kW (100 MW)
- Module type: 0 (Standard)
- Array type: 2 (Single-axis tracking)
- DC-to-AC ratio: 1.3
- Tilt: 0 degrees
- Azimuth: 180 degrees
- Inverter efficiency: 96%
- System losses: 14.08%
- Ground coverage ratio (GCR): 0.3
- Annual degradation: 0.5%/year

Financial Parameters:
- Analysis period: 25 years
- Inflation rate: 2.5%
- Real discount rate: 6.4%
- Federal income tax rate: 21%
- State income tax rate: 8.84%
- Total installed cost: $100,000,000
- Annual O&M (fixed): $15/kW-yr ($1,500,000/year)
- Federal ITC: 30%
- MACRS depreciation: 5-year schedule, federal and state
- Debt fraction: 60%
- Debt interest rate: 5.5% (annual)
- Debt term: 18 years
- PPA price (year 1): $0.035/kWh with 1.5% annual escalation
- Salvage value: 0

Use the TMY weather file for Daggett, CA bundled with the SAM installation.

Save the simulation results to a JSON file exactly at: /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/daggett_100mw_finance.json

The JSON must contain the following keys exactly: 'year1_energy_kwh', 'capacity_factor_percent', 'lcoe_real_cents_per_kwh', 'lcoe_nom_cents_per_kwh', 'project_irr_aftertax_percent', 'equity_irr_aftertax_percent', 'npv_aftertax_dollars', 'min_dscr', 'ppa_price_year1_cents_per_kwh', 'debt_fraction_percent', 'total_installed_cost_dollars', 'cashflow_aftertax' (array of 26 values), and 'annual_energy_kwh' (array of 25 values).
